text: Who am I here with today? Tom Brady. The GOAT. The Greatest of All Time. Appreciate it. So I want to challenge you. Take me to the most life-changing conversation you ever had. What is the greatest piece of advice you ever received throughout your career? When I was a sophomore in college, I had a sports psychologist mentor in mind. And I would go and bitch and complain to him all the time that I wasn't getting the same opportunities that other guys were. And he sat me down in his office one Tuesday afternoon and he said, Why don't you quit bitching about all the things you're not getting and worry about the things you are getting? And don't worry about anybody else. So many times we focus on what everyone else gets and we lose track of what we're getting and the opportunities we're getting. And if they give you three opportunities and a practice to go out there and do your best, then do it. Take accountability. Nobody's coming to save you. Accountability is a word that I think we need to ingrain in all of us all the time. I always tell everybody, you have to get outside of your comfort zone. To comfort zones where dreams go to die. You're 100% right. Well said. Did you have anybody that doubted you? Yeah, about 32 teams for the first six rounds of the NFL draft. But you didn't listen to them. You know what? I think you have to listen to what's inside of you. Take me back to Super Bowl 51. You were down. My favorite games. You were down 28-3 at halftime. The biggest deficit in Super Bowl history. Yeah. It came back in one. What were you telling yourself? What was that self-talk? Go fight your ass off. We're down 21-3 and Coach Belichick said to us, 21 points won't be enough to beat us today. We show what we're all about and we didn't stop fighting. Don't quit. If you quit, you're a loser for life. One of my favorite videos I've ever seen of you is when you were asked who your hero is. Who is your hero? I said then, I'll say now my dad's my hero. I am who I am because of my dad. You have a couple sons. Yeah. Give me a lesson that your dad taught you that you were going to instill in your kids one day. You know, my dad was a rock. And he had the most belief in me of anyone. And that's what I really want to pass on to my kids. I believe in them. Whatever they set their mind to, they're going to accomplish. Tom, if me and you died tomorrow and you had one more guiding principle to lead with the younger generation, what would that be? Be proud of the person you look back in the mirror every single day because that's the person you got to satisfy. And the life you lead is the lesson you teach. So when you wake up every day and the choices that you make, you don't have to say anything. If you want to live a life of integrity, be that. If you want to live a life of resilience, be that. If you want to live a life of caring and joy, be that. That's what you can bring to the world. You got an amazing advice, Tom. Great to meet you. I appreciate you. That was amazing. Seriously. Thanks man.
